paper_title: The use of larvae and pupal exuviae to study the biodiversity of Chironomidae in Mediterranean streams

methods.method_name: Field Sample Collection (Larvae and Pupal Exuviae)
methods.description: Describes the process of collecting larvae and pupal exuviae from streams in Catalonia using specific protocols and equipment during different seasons.

steps.description: We collected samples of larvae and pupal exuviae in 25 sites in Catalonia (NE Spain) in three seasons: spring, summer and autumn (Table 1) with a total of 38 samples, in which both larvae and pupae were found.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect biological samples (larvae, pupae) from specified stream sites and seasons
outputs: raw biological samples (larvae and pupal exuviae)

steps.description: Samples of pupal skins were taken with a hand net with a mesh size of 250 microns, using the chironomid pupal exuviae technique (CPET) field protocol.This method entails searching for exuviae in areas of the river where foam had previously accumulated (Rieradevall & Prat, 1986b;Raunio et al., 2007) and in other areas where the exuvia may accumulate (e.g., emerged macrophytes).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect pupal exuviae using hand net and CPET protocol from specific stream areas

steps.description: For larvae we followed the sampling method described in the Guadalmed protocol (Jaimez et al., 2002), using a hand net of 250 microns; in this case intensive sampling was carried out and macroinvertebrate families ide ntified in the field.Several samples were taken until no new families of macroinvertebrates appeared.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect larvae using hand net and Guadalmed protocol with intensive sampling until taxonomic richness plateaus

methods.description: Describes the process of preserving collected samples in formalin and transporting them to the laboratory for further processing.

steps.description: Samples were preserved in formalin (4%) and transported to the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: Preserve collected biological samples in formalin
inputs: raw biological samples (larvae and pupal exuviae)

steps.description: Samples were preserved in formalin (4%) and transported to the laboratory.

methods.description: Describes the laboratory procedures for sorting, examining, classifying, and preparing pupal exuviae samples for microscopic observation.

steps.description: All the pupal exuviae were sorted, examined, classified to the genera level under the stereoscope and at least 10 individuals of each genera or morphotype were prepared for observation under the microscope.

All the pupal exuviae were sorted, examined, classified to the genera level under the stereoscope and at least 10 individuals of each genera or morphotype were prepared for observation under the microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine sorted pupal exuviae using stereoscope

steps.description: All the pupal exuviae were sorted, examined, classified to the genera level under the stereoscope and at least 10 individuals of each genera or morphotype were prepared for observation under the microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify pupal exuviae to genera level using stereoscope
outputs: classified pupal exuviae (genera level or morphotype)

steps.description: All the pupal exuviae were sorted, examined, classified to the genera level under the stereoscope and at least 10 individuals of each genera or morphotype were prepared for observation under the microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare microscope slides for observation of pupal exuviae (at least 10 individuals per genera/morphotype)

methods.description: Describes the laboratory procedures for sorting, observing, identifying, and preparing larvae samples for microscopic observation.

steps.description: For larvae, between 100 and 602 individuals (Table 2) were sorted and observed under the stereoscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Sort larvae from samples (specific count range per sample)

steps.description: For larvae, between 100 and 602 individuals (Table 2) were sorted and observed under the stereoscope.
Larvae were initially identified following the morphotype key of Prat & Rieradevall (2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Initially identify larvae using a specific morphotype key

steps.description: All the larvae of the least abundant morphotypes were mounted onto slides, while at least 10 larvae were prepared for the most abundant ones.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare microscope slides for all larvae of least abundant morphotypes

steps.description: All the larvae of the least abundant morphotypes were mounted onto slides, while at least 10 larvae were prepared for the most abundant ones.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare larvae samples for observation (at least 10 individuals per most abundant morphotype)
